qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed XML with duplicate expanded-name attributes is accepted and the later attacker-controlled value wins
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a parse-time duplicate expanded-name attribute bug in `lib/sax.js`/`lib/dom-parser.js`/`lib/dom.js`, while the advisory is a serializer-time `DocumentType` injection in `createDocumentType` and the DOCTYPE branch of `XMLSerializer.serializeToString`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable `DocumentType` serialization root cause. The only qualifying finding concerns duplicate expanded-name attributes during XML parsing, not unvalidated `publicId`/`systemId`/`internalSubset` serialization.
evidence: The only final qualifying finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` starts at `DOMParser.parseFromString(...)` and relies on duplicate expanded-name handling in `repo/lib/sax.js`, `repo/lib/dom-parser.js`, and `repo/lib/dom.js`.

The advisory root cause is `DOMImplementation.createDocumentType(...)` storing `publicId`, `systemId`, and `internalSubset` verbatim and the DOCTYPE serializer branch in `lib/dom.js` emitting those fields without validation or escaping.

The advisory text explicitly states that the parse path is safe and that reachability is only through programmatic `createDocumentType` calls or direct `DocumentType` property writes, which is a different source-to-sink path from the finding.
